description: Trigger and behavior cases for manual test case authoring: stranger-executable cases with exact data, one observable expected result per step, checkpoints, verdict rules, cleanup.

cases.prompt: Write manual test cases for the workspace invitation flow so our contractors can run them each release.

assertions: Produces cases with ids, requirement traces, exact roles, environments, preconditions, and literal test data values.
Writes numbered steps with exactly one action and one observable expected result each — no 'verify it works'.
Covers happy, negative (already-member, expired invite), and boundary paths; marks screenshot checkpoints with masking notes.
Defines pass/fail/blocked semantics and cleanup returning the environment to neutral.

cases.prompt: Turn these acceptance criteria for the export feature into a manual verification pass. One criterion says 'export should be fast'.

assertions: Converts testable criteria into traced cases and flags 'fast' as untestable, returning it for measurable restatement instead of writing a judgment-free case.
Keeps steps implementation-agnostic (screen outcomes, not API/DB internals).

cases.prompt: Go click through the invitation flow on staging right now and tell me if it's broken.

assertions: Executing a live session is clickthrough-test-engineer; this skill authors the reusable case documents.

cases.prompt: Write quick manual test cases — keep them short, just 'open the page and check everything looks right'.

assertions: Rejects 'check everything looks right' as an expected result and writes observable, criterion-based outcomes instead.
Explains the stranger-executability bar rather than shipping vague cases on request.
